question.text: Which of the following entities is NOT part of the three-tiered regulatory framework for commodity markets?

answer.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The three tiers are: Government of India, SEBI, and Exchanges. RBI is not directly part of this framework (though it regulates banks which participate in commodity markets). **The Trap:** Government, SEBI, and Exchanges are all part of the framework. RBI has an indirect role but is not one of the three tiers. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I covers RBI's role in currency derivatives (where RBI IS part of the regulatory framework). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** RBI becomes relevant for commodity market participants when they use bank finance, foreign exchange for international commodity trades, or when monetary policy affects commodity prices.
